title: Skin infection follow-up

red_flags.text: Supports documenting clinician-identified serious illness, periorbital location, unusual exposure, treatment non-response, lymphangitis, oral-intake limitation, and an already made referral or specialist discussion; it does not generate urgency.
red_flags.rationale: The exact clinician-referral section supports documenting a clinician-entered plan, safety net, arranged follow-up and patient questions only; it does not generate escalation.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports documenting clinician-identified serious illness, periorbital location, unusual exposure, treatment non-response, lymphangitis, oral-intake limitation, and an already made referral or specialist discussion; it does not generate urgency.

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.1.13–1.1.14 — clinician referral and specialist advice criteria

treatment_and_management.text: Supports documenting rapid or significant worsening, lack of improvement, systemic illness, pain out of proportion, spread, underlying conditions, serious alternative diagnoses, and microbiology results during clinician reassessment.
treatment_and_management.rationale: The exact skin-infection reassessment section supports documenting interval history, change, symptoms, clinician skin inspection, distribution or extent, general appearance, secondary infection signs, severe pain, immune context and already available microbiology without asserting cellulitis, erysipelas, or treatment failure.
treatment_and_management.evidence_extract: Supports documenting rapid or significant worsening, lack of improvement, systemic illness, pain out of proportion, spread, underlying conditions, serious alternative diagnoses, and microbiology results during clinician reassessment.

treatment_and_management.text: Supports documenting skin redness and swelling extent, site, severity, broken skin, penetrating or water exposure, microbiology already obtained, skin-tone visibility, and treatment already prescribed without generating diagnosis or treatment.
treatment_and_management.rationale: Site, extent, exposure, broken-skin, skin-tone and previous-treatment context was reviewed for follow-up applicability.
treatment_and_management.evidence_extract: Supports documenting skin redness and swelling extent, site, severity, broken skin, penetrating or water exposure, microbiology already obtained, skin-tone visibility, and treatment already prescribed without generating diagnosis or treatment.

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.1.8 — alternative causes, extent, site, severity, exposure, and review context

sources_used.title: Cellulitis and erysipelas: antimicrobial prescribing
sources_used.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE adaptation

source_limitations: Exact infection type, site and dimensions, exposure, diagnosis, previous medicine and adherence, response, culture result interpretation, antibiotic, dose, route, duration, admission, referral, and review interval remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
